library_routing.fallback: Static table of coefficient tail exponents.
library_routing.why: Frequency weights give a direct finite model of weak derivative control for p=2.

library_routing.fallback: Pure SymPy identity and numeric norm ledger.
library_routing.why: The flat multiplier symbol is visible as a heatmap and its norm identity is exact.
The inverse Laplacian is most transparent as high-frequency damping.

library_routing.fallback: Ordered ledger with edge list checks.
library_routing.why: The proof is a dependency chain; a directed acyclic graph exposes what each estimate unlocks.

library_routing.fallback: CSV ladder and static Matplotlib plot.
library_routing.why: The iteration depends on p and q; Plotly makes the threshold crossing inspectable, with a PNG fallback.

visuals.inspection_target: Which weighted energies stabilize and which keep growing.
Second derivative multipliers are bounded away from the zero mode.
The solution tail drops by k^2 compared with the forcing tail.
The path from weak equation to smooth curve has no circular dependency.
The r exponent crosses 2 after finitely many improvements of q.
